summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Email Reminder Toggle Bug Fix. All 21 backend tests passed (100%). The bug fix correctly prevents reminder emails from being sent when reminders are disabled by user.
passed_tests: Scenario 1: Create event with reminders enabled - reminders_enabled=True, flags=False
Scenario 2: Disable reminders sets flags to True - CRITICAL BUG FIX VERIFIED
Scenario 3: Re-enable with date change resets flags to False
Scenario 4: Frontend boolean handling works correctly for True/False
Scenario 5: Database query uses explicit $eq: True check
Edge case: Toggle reminders multiple times works correctly
Edge case: Non-interview event types have reminder fields
Edge case: Updating other fields doesn't affect reminder flags
Auth: User registration
Auth: User login
Calendar: Create basic event with reminder fields
Calendar: Create event with interview_type
Calendar: Create event with meeting_link
Calendar: Create event with job_application_id
Calendar: Create event with reminders disabled
Calendar: Get all events with reminder fields
Calendar: Update event with new fields
Calendar: Delete event
Email: Send test reminder endpoint
Email: Notification logs endpoint
Interview Prep: Get history endpoint

fix_details.fix: When reminders_enabled is set to False, sets reminder_24hr_sent=True and reminder_1hr_sent=True
fix_details.purpose: Prevents scheduler from sending reminders for disabled events

fix_details.fix: Uses explicit {"$eq": True} check for reminders_enabled in MongoDB query
fix_details.purpose: Only fetches events where reminders are explicitly enabled

fix_details.fix: Re-fetches event from database and checks reminders_enabled before sending
fix_details.purpose: Prevents race conditions where user disables reminders after initial query

fix_details.fix: When start_date changes, resets reminder flags to False
fix_details.purpose: Allows new reminders to be sent for rescheduled events

fix_details.fix: Uses Boolean(formData.reminders_enabled) to ensure boolean value is sent
fix_details.purpose: Ensures correct boolean type is sent to backend

critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix implementation is correct and comprehensive
All three protection layers are in place: 1) Flag setting on disable, 2) Explicit query filter, 3) Re-check before send
Date change handling correctly resets flags for rescheduled events
Frontend correctly sends boolean values to backend

test_credentials.note: Multiple test users created during testing with pattern test_*@example.com

seed_data_creation: Test calendar events and users created during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email reminder toggle bug fix has been verified. The fix prevents reminder emails from being sent when reminders are disabled by: 1) Setting reminder flags to True when disabling, 2) Using explicit $eq: True in MongoDB queries, 3) Re-checking reminders_enabled before sending. All 21 tests pass.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The previous implementation had three issues: 1) When reminders were disabled via update, the reminder_24hr_sent and reminder_1hr_sent flags were not set to True, allowing the scheduler to still pick up these events. 2) The MongoDB query didn't use explicit $eq: True, potentially matching events with missing or null reminders_enabled field. 3) The process_reminder function didn't re-check the database before sending, allowing race conditions.
reproduction_steps: 1. Create a calendar event with reminders_enabled=True
2. Update the event to set reminders_enabled=False
3. Wait for the scheduler to run (every 5 minutes)
4. Previously: Reminder would still be sent because flags were False
5. Now: Reminder is NOT sent because flags are set to True when disabling
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: The fix implements three layers of protection: 1) Setting reminder flags to True when disabling prevents scheduler from picking up the event. 2) Using explicit $eq: True in queries ensures only explicitly enabled events are fetched. 3) Re-fetching and re-checking before sending prevents race conditions.
